memes.label: About This Wiki

memes.label: The British Museum Reading Room
memes.description: The great domed reading room of the British Museum, where Henry Ryecroft pursued classical scholarship during his years of poverty in London, finding intellectual sustenance amid physical deprivation.

memes.label: Devon
memes.description: The rural county in southwest England where Henry Ryecroft found his final home and the setting of his tranquil retirement after decades of poverty in London.

memes.label: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
memes.description: German poet, dramatist, and polymath (1749–1832), cited by Henry Ryecroft as exemplar of the born artist whose creative spontaneity transcends method.

memes.label: Literature and the Classics
memes.description: Henry Ryecroft's lifelong devotion to classical and European literature, which sustained him through poverty and enriched his years of retirement in Devon.

memes.label: Ryecroft's First Visit to Devon
memes.description: The transformative journey to Devon that awakened Henry Ryecroft's dormant sensibilities and prefigured his permanent retreat from London literary life.

memes.label: The Diamond Jubilee (1897)
memes.description: Queen Victoria's Diamond Jubilee of 1897, observed by Henry Ryecroft with a characteristic mixture of sympathy for popular celebration and elegiac reflection on the passing of an age.

memes.label: The Seasons in Ryecroft
memes.description: The organizing principle of Henry Ryecroft's private papers, in which the cycle of spring, summer, autumn, and winter structures both the narrative and the author's philosophical reflections on nature, mortality, and contentment.

memes.label: The Secondhand Bookstalls of London
memes.description: The secondhand bookshops and street stalls of Victorian London — particularly those in Holywell Street and Goodge Street — where Henry Ryecroft acquired his library through sacrifice and discovered the material culture of books.

memes.label: The Private Papers of Henry Ryecroft
memes.description: The Private Papers of Henry Ryecroft — Front Matter

memes.label: Tottenham Court Road
memes.description: A London thoroughfare and the surrounding district where Henry Ryecroft lodged in desperate poverty during his early literary career, later recalled with unexpected affection.

memes.label: William Shakespeare
memes.description: English playwright and poet (1564–1616), revered by Henry Ryecroft as the supreme literary master whose works served as lifelong companions through poverty and leisure alike.
